ddls:
CREATE TABLE "developers" (
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each developer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the developer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the developer */,
  "github_profile" TEXT /* GitHub profile URL of the developer */,
  "linkedin_profile" TEXT /* LinkedIn profile URL of the developer */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief bio or description of the developer */,
  "avatar_url" TEXT /* URL to the developer's avatar or profile picture */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the developer's account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the developer's account was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the developer is currently active */,
  "primary_language" TEXT /* Primary programming language used by the developer */,
  "secondary_language" TEXT /* Secondary programming language used by the developer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "github_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity record */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "commits" INTEGER /* Number of commits made by the developer */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers of the developer */,
  "following" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the developer is following */,
  "pull_requests" INTEGER /* Number of pull requests made by the developer */,
  "issues_opened" INTEGER /* Number of issues opened by the developer */,
  "issues_closed" INTEGER /* Number of issues closed by the developer */,
  "stars_given" INTEGER /* Number of stars given to repositories by the developer */,
  "forks_given" INTEGER /* Number of forks given to repositories by the developer */,
  "repos_contributed" INTEGER /* Number of repositories the developer has contributed to */,
  "last_activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the developer's last activity on GitHub */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_github_activity_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "linkedin_profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each LinkedIn profile */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "current_position" TEXT /* Current job position of the developer */,
  "current_company" TEXT /* Current company of the developer */,
  "experience" TEXT /* JSON array of past job experiences */,
  "education" TEXT /* JSON array of educational backgrounds */,
  "skills" TEXT /* JSON array of skills */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* JSON array of recommendations received */,
  "public_profile_url" TEXT /* URL to the public LinkedIn profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_linkedin_profiles_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the developer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the developer */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the developer's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the developer's location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State or province of the developer */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the developer */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the developer's location */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the location information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "affiliations" (
  "affiliation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each affiliation */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "affiliation_name" TEXT /* Name of the affiliation */,
  "affiliation_type" TEXT /* Type of affiliation (e.g., company, project, organization) */,
  "affiliation_start_date" TEXT /* Date when the affiliation started */,
  "affiliation_end_date" TEXT /* Date when the affiliation ended */,
  "affiliation_role" TEXT /* Role of the developer in the affiliation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("affiliation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_affiliations_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "new_email" TEXT /* New email address of the developer */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the email update */,
  "update_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the email update */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who updated the email */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the new email is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_updates_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "github_commits" (
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each commit */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "commit_date" TEXT /* Date of the commit */,
  "commit_message" TEXT /* Message of the commit */,
  "commit_sha" TEXT /* SHA hash of the commit */,
  "commit_author_name" TEXT /* Name of the commit author */,
  "commit_author_email" TEXT /* Email of the commit author */,
  "commit_branch" TEXT /* Branch where the commit was made */,
  "commit_repo" TEXT /* Repository where the commit was made */,
  "commit_files" TEXT /* JSON array of files changed in the commit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("commit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_github_commits_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "linkedin_connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "connection_name" TEXT /* Name of the connection */,
  "connection_title" TEXT /* Title of the connection */,
  "connection_company" TEXT /* Company of the connection */,
  "connection_location" TEXT /* Location of the connection */,
  "connection_industry" TEXT /* Industry of the connection */,
  "connection_connection_date" TEXT /* Date when the connection was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_linkedin_connections_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gender_info" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender record */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the developer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the developer */,
  "pronouns" TEXT /* Preferred pronouns of the developer */,
  "self_identified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the gender is self-identified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gender_info_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)
